title: [PDF] Commercial Bank Examination Manual, 3000—CAPITAL
summary: This is Section 3000 of the Federal Reserve's Commercial Bank Examination Manual covering capital adequacy assessment procedures for bank examiners, effective November 2020. The document describes Regulation Q requirements, components of regulatory capital (CET1, Additional Tier 1, Tier 2), and examination procedures for assessing bank financial condition. This is an internal supervisory manual, not a source that discusses macro catalysts, market-moving events, or trading implications.
key_points: Regulation Q (12 CFR Part 217) establishes risk-based capital requirements, market risk requirements, and leverage requirements for Board-regulated institutions
Regulatory capital has three components: Common Equity Tier 1 (CET1), Additional Tier 1, and Tier 2
CET1 is the highest-quality capital, primarily composed of common stock and retained earnings
Risk-based capital ratios are calculated by dividing qualifying capital by risk-weighted assets
Capital adequacy triggers the most supervisory action under the Prompt Corrective Action (PCA) provisions of Section 38 of the Federal Deposit Insurance Act
Regulation Q implements Basel Committee standards and addresses weaknesses highlighted during the 2008-09 financial crisis
why_it_matters: This document is a supervisory examination manual for Federal Reserve examiners assessing bank capital adequacy. It is not relevant to macro catalysts or market structure in the trading sense. It has no market-moving content and is intended for bank regulatory supervisors, not market participants. Recommend rejection from macro catalysts archive.
